purpose: Tests strong direct match behavior, recent evidence weighting, title alignment, and safe high-coverage tailoring opportunities across summary, skills, and bullets.
key_expected_signals: All must-have requirements should match with recent experience evidence.
Title alignment should be strong and summary-safe.
Tailoring can strengthen keyword frequency and section coverage without fabrication.
key_risk_areas: Frequency balancing should avoid overusing already strong terms.
Bullet rewrites should stay additive and not duplicate existing phrasing too aggressively.

purpose: Tests proceed behavior when the candidate is strong overall but some target terms are weakly surfaced, older, or present only in skills and projects.
key_expected_signals: Most must-have requirements should match, but some preferred terms should remain missing.
Summary optimization should have clear room to add supported job language.
Bullet rewrites should improve evidence surfacing for FastAPI, React, and TypeScript without inventing newer experience.
key_risk_areas: The planner should not overstate project-only or older experience as core recent ownership.
Skills optimization should normalize variants such as JS, ReactJS, and Postgres naturally.

purpose: Tests threshold behavior when alignment is partial, relevant evidence is stale, and current role support is weaker than older experience.
key_expected_signals: A subset of must-have requirements should match, but not enough for a clear proceed.
Recency logic should prefer older evidence cautiously and avoid overstating present-day fit.
Title alignment should be partial rather than exact.
key_risk_areas: The system should remain conservative about rewriting current-role bullets with stale technical terms.
Summary and skills optimization should not imply stronger front-end or cloud depth than the resume supports.

purpose: Tests skip behavior, missing hard technical alignment, title mismatch, and conservative planning when only limited surface overlap exists.
key_expected_signals: Only a small portion of must-have requirements should match.
Overlap should cluster around QA-adjacent tooling such as Python and CI/CD rather than platform engineering delivery.
Tailoring opportunities should be limited and conservative.
key_risk_areas: Unsupported platform terms must not be invented in bullets or summary.
The planner should avoid turning testing or release work into backend product engineering claims.

purpose: Tests keyword inflation, skills-only overlap, weak experience support, and anti-fabrication constraints for a resume that can mislead naive ATS matching.
key_expected_signals: Summary and skills sections should contain many overlapping terms with little experience backing.
Evidence linking should mark several terms as skills-only or under-supported.
Title alignment should remain weak because the resume is solutions-oriented rather than platform-engineering oriented.
key_risk_areas: Weak systems may over-score this profile because of summary and skills keyword density.
Tailoring must not promote unsupported implementation claims into bullets, summary, or reordered skills.
